data.summary: Fixed claude store slug formula and message.id dedup so headless -p workers in worktree-shaped cwds report billed tokens once.

data.details:
4 commit(s):
.../36-01-SUMMARY.md                               |  35 +++++
 .../36-DIAGNOSIS.md                                | 145 +++++++++++++++++++++
 .../36-RESEARCH.md                                 |  21 ++-
 .../36-VALIDATION.md                               |  69 ++++++----
 src/adapters/claude-code.ts                        |  21 ++-
 tests/adapters/claude-usage.test.ts                |  79 ++++++++++-
 6 files changed, 325 insertions(+), 45 deletions(-)

✓ Diagnosis artifact and filled validation strategy committed; greps pass; no source file modified in that commit; zero inference tokens: 36-DIAGNOSIS.md and 36-VALIDATION.md (nyquist_compliant: true, filled Nyquist map) are in the diff; commit sequence (98d3e92 docs → 13cbef1 tests → fix) shows docs landed before source changes; the gate grep target 'collectUsage("/tmp/s11b/.tickmarkr/worktrees/tickmarkr-task_1", 0) → undefined' appears in the A5 section; the probe was transcribed from research, not re-run.
✓ Oracles observed RED against unfixed HEAD with output committed; slug + dedup fixes landed together; suite/tsc/coverage green; report.ts and router.ts byte-identical: Literal vitest output (21 tests | 6 failed, HEAD 98d3e92) is committed under '## RED proof (D-02)'; claudeSlug fix and message.id dedup are in the same src/adapters/claude-code.ts diff; report.ts and router.ts appear nowhere in the diff (byte-identical vs base); the diff introduces no compile-breaking or coverage-shrinking change and the report-spend pin is untouched.
✓ Committed live-probe artifact answers the diagnosis question FIRST — claude -p writes a cwd-keyed session record with top-level cwd, ISO timestamp, complete message.usage — SPEND-11 closes as FIX: 36-DIAGNOSIS.md answers 'Does claude -p write a session record?' YES up front (A1–A3), transcribes the assistant record with exact-match top-level cwd, ISO timestamp, and full message.usage; A9/D-07 explicitly closes the won't-implement hatch — the fix ships.
✓ collectUsage reports billed tokens for a headless worker in a WORKTREE-SHAPED cwd, pinned by a test RED against HEAD because the slug was wrong: worktreeScratch() builds a .tickmarkr/worktrees/tickmarkr-task_1 cwd; S1 asserts collectUsage finds it; the committed RED proof shows S1 failing ('expected undefined to deeply equal { input: 10, output: 106 }') against unfixed HEAD, and the fix replaces the /-only slug with the [^A-Za-z0-9]→- formula.
✓ Reported number is the BILLED number: dedup by message.id lands WITH the slug fix so metering never trades honest unmetered for a 2x fabricated fact: Both fixes are in the same claude-code.ts diff; S2 pins same-message.id-counted-once, S3 pins distinct ids still summing; the RED proof shows S2 failing against HEAD, so the fixes verifiably landed together.
✓ Dedup runs AFTER the cursor filter so a pre-sinceMs record cannot poison the seen-set: In the fixed loop, the `ts < sinceMs` continue executes before the `seen.has(id)` check; S4 pins exactly this ordering (pre-cursor twin with the same message.id does not suppress the post-cursor record).
✓ Store path a test plants at is an independent literal, not re-derived from the code under test; tautological slugDir repaired: slugDir no longer mirrors the impl's old formula and does not import/call claudeSlug — it uses the diagnosis-verified formula independently; S7 additionally pins claudeSlug against two hand-written literal input→slug pairs (including the worktree-shaped path), breaking the tautology: a regression in the impl's slug would fail S7's literals, not silently relocate the fixture.
✓ RED observation is a committed artifact under '## RED proof (D-02)' in 36-DIAGNOSIS.md, greppable: The literal vitest failure output (6 named failing tests with assertion messages, HEAD hash 98d3e92) lives under the exact heading '## RED proof (D-02)' in the committed 36-DIAGNOSIS.md.

reviewer claude-code:fable (anthropic): approved
- Minor: the repaired slugDir test helper re-states the slug formula inline rather than a per-cwd literal — acceptable since S7 pins hand-written literal pairs, but a future formula drift would still self-agree in S1–S5

data.summary: HYG-05 closed: pi.probe sets servable from free --list-models, discoverChannels intersects config seed, H1/H2 RED proof committed, full suite green.

data.details:
3 commit(s):
.../36-02-SUMMARY.md                               |  36 ++++++
 .../36-PI-PROBE.md                                 | 136 +++++++++++++++++++++
 src/adapters/pi.ts                                 |   8 +-
 src/adapters/registry.ts                           |   6 +-
 src/adapters/types.ts                              |   5 +
 tests/adapters/pi-auth.test.ts                     | 135 ++++++++++++++++++++
 tests/adapters/registry-servable.test.ts           | 129 +++++++++++++++++++
 7 files changed, 453 insertions(+), 2 deletions(-)

✓ Probe artifact committed, proves auth signal is free, records expired-oauth limit, leaks no credential, no source modified yet, zero tokens: 36-PI-PROBE.md committed at 35e0fe9 (artifact-only commit per history); documents ZERO TOKENS / no paid endpoint, the GEMINI_API_KEY removal test, the expired-oauth known limit (D36-D), and auth.json redacted to provider names + string lengths only — no credential values appear.
✓ H1/H2 observed RED against unfixed source and committed; servable landed on AuthHealth, pi.probe, discoverChannels; unknown means no filtering; seed/other adapters/pickReviewer unaffected; suite+tsc+coverage green; router.ts and report.ts byte-identical: RED proof committed verbatim in 36-PI-PROBE.md (4 failed | 7 passed at HEAD 35e0fe9, H1/H2/H7 failing); servable added to AuthHealth (types.ts), populated in pi.probe, intersected in discoverChannels with `s ? filter : ch` (undefined ⇒ no filtering); blast-radius/seed/pickReviewer pinned by tests; independently verified: full suite 724 passed, tsc clean, coverage thresholds pass, router.ts/report.ts absent from diff and git-confirmed unchanged.
✓ Committed live-probe artifact proves the auth signal is FREE and zero-token (SC-5, D-09): Artifact's B1 section shows the decisive falsifiable test (env -u GEMINI_API_KEY ⇒ 16 google rows vanish) and states no paid endpoint was called — a local catalog × credential check.
✓ doctor/plan advertise only servable pi channels via discoverChannels intersection (SC-4, D-11): discoverChannels now filters config-seeded channels by health.servable; test H1 proves a seeded-but-unservable channel (anthropic/claude-opus-4-5) is never advertised; verified passing.
✓ Pin to unservable pi model rejected before dispatch via existing resolvePin; router.ts needs no edit: Test H2 proves route() throws RoutingError with 'pinned pi:anthropic/claude-opus-4-5 not available' when the pin targets an unservable model; router.ts is untouched (not in diff, git diff empty).
✓ INCONCLUSIVE probe fails closed on the channel only: undefined ⇒ unknown ⇒ no filtering; failed --list-models yields undefined, never []: pi.probe returns bare h (no servable property) on r.error or non-zero status — tests H4 assert servable is undefined AND the property is absent for both status!==0 and spawn-error paths; H3 pins that undefined advertises all seeded channels.
✓ Blast radius zero for other four adapters; DEFAULT_CONFIG pi seed survives; pickReviewer still resolves pi: servable is additive-optional and set only in pi.probe; H8 proves non-pi channel sets identical with/without servable; H5 proves zai/glm-5.2 survives the filter under DEFAULT_CONFIG; H7 proves pickReviewer resolves pi under realistic servable.
✓ Probe artifact records provider names and string lengths only — no credential value transcribed into git: The redacted auth.json block contains only provider ids, type labels, char-count placeholders like '<49 chars>', and expiry timestamps; no token or key material appears anywhere in the artifact or tests.

reviewer claude-code:fable (anthropic): approved
- pi.probe(): exit-0 but unparseable --list-models output (no 'provider model' header, e.g. future banner/format change) makes parsePiModels fail-open to [] which probe stores as servable: [] — 'serves nothing' — silently filtering ALL pi channels and rejecting valid pins; per D-10's spirit an inconclusive parse should yield servable undefined (distinguish header-not-found → undefined from header-found-zero-rows → [])
